summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that deleting the .env file bypasses the install guard and allows the installer to run again, creating a new admin account and taking over the application.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should delete the .env file, access the /install endpoint, configure a new database and admin account, demonstrating complete application takeover through install guard bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh demonstrated that after removing .env, the install flow becomes accessible and creates a new admin account with attacker-controlled credentials, confirming the install guard bypass.
verification_method: The run script verifies that a new admin account was created and can log in with the attacker-specified credentials, confirming successful application takeover.
evidence_produced_by_run: The .env file was successfully deleted, bypassing the install guard.
The /install endpoint became accessible and accepted new configuration.
A new admin account was created with attacker-controlled credentials.
The new admin account can successfully log in, confirming complete takeover.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
